LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF CALIFORNIA CORRECTIONAL PEACE OFFICERS ASSOCIATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. FAILURE TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR 5759

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DAVID GILB MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF, AND PLAINTIFF FAILS TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. THE RATIONAL BASIS TEST APPLIES TO THIS CASE. THERE IS NO EVIDENCE OF DISCRIMINATORY INTENT ON THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION. THE STATE HAD NO CHOICE BUT TO PROVIDE COVERAGE TO NON-UNION EMPLOYEES ONCE THEY WERE DROPPED FROM PARTICIPATION BY THE TRUST FUND THROUGH NO FAULT OF THE STATE. THE STATE MADE COVERAGE AVAILABLE THROUGH EXISTING CONTRACTS. PLAINTIFF CITES NO CASE LAW OR EVIDENCE THAT CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT UNDER THE FACTORS SET FORTH IN ARLINGTON HEIGHTS VS. METROPOLITAN HOUSING DEVELOPMENT CORP. (1977) 429 U.S. 252. PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION ALSO FAIL. DEFENDANT SHIFTS THE BURDEN BY SHOWING THERE IS NO PROOF OF DIRECT AND SUBSTANTIAL INTERFERENCE WITH AN INDIVIDUAL'S ABILITY TO JOIN A UNION. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PRESENT ANY EVIDENCE OR CASE LAW TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT THAT THE STATE'S ACTIONS DIRECTLY AND SUBSTANTIALLY INTERFERED WITH THE RIGHT TO ASSOCIATE. (COUNSEL TO ATTACHMENT STATEMENT OF FACTS TO PROPOSED ORDER.) (COUNSEL WILL MEET AND CONFER RE: PROPOSED ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR 5759

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Jun-12-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT DAVID GILB MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: Gregg McLean Adam, of Carroll, Burdick & McDonough LLP (415) 989-5900, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Christopher E. Thomas, Esq. Labor Relations Counsel, Dept. of Personnel Adm., State of CA (916) 324-0512, appearing for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant's motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant shifted the burden of proof, and plaintiff fails to raise a triable issue of fact. The rational basis test applies to this case. There is no evidence of discriminatory intent on the first cause of action. The State had no choice but to provide coverage to non-union employees once they were dropped from participation by the Trust Fund through no fault of the State. The State made coverage available through existing contracts. Plaintiff cites no case law or evidence that creates a triable issue of fact under the factors set forth in Arlington Heights vs. Metropolitan Housing Development Corp. (1977) 429 U.S. 252. Plaintiff's second and third causes of action also fail. Defendant shifts the burden by showing there is no proof of direct and substantial interference with an individual's ability to join a union. Plaintiff fails to present any evidence or case law to create a triable issue of fact that the State's actions directly and substantially interfered with the right to associate. (Counsel to attachment statement of facts to proposed Order.) (Counsel will meet and confer re: Proposed Order.)(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-12-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF CALIFORNIA CORRECTIONAL PEACE OFFICERS ASSOCIATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/or in the alternative summary adjudication. Appearances: Gregg McLean Adam, of Carroll, Burdick & McDonough LLP (415) 989-5900, appearing for Plaintiff and moving party; Christopher E. Thomas, Esq. Labor Relations Counsel, Dept. of Personnel Adm., State of CA (916) 324-0512, appearing for Defendant and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff's motion for summary judgment is denied. Failure to shift burden of proof. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
